mission.title: Install and Configure Flux on Kubernetes
mission.description: Flux is an open and extensible continuous delivery solution for Kubernetes, leveraging GitOps principles to keep clusters in sync with configuration sources. Installing Flux allows you to automate updates and manage your Kubernetes applications effectively.

steps.title: Add the FluxCD Helm repository
steps.description:
Add the official FluxCD community Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.
```bash
helm repo add fluxcd-community https://fluxcd-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update
```

steps.title: Install Flux using Helm
steps.description:
Install Flux in the 'flux-system' namespace using the official flux2 Helm chart.
```bash
helm install flux fluxcd-community/flux2 --namespace flux-system --create-namespace
```

steps.title: Verify the installation
steps.description:
Check if the Flux pods are running successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

steps.title: Post-install configuration
steps.description:
Verify that all Flux components are healthy and ready.
```bash
kubectl get deployments --namespace flux-system
```

uninstall.title: Remove the Helm release
uninstall.description:
Uninstall the Flux release from the cluster.
```bash
helm uninstall flux --namespace flux-system
```

uninstall.title: Clean up CRDs and resources
uninstall.description:
Delete any remaining custom resource definitions related to Flux.
```bash
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep fluxcd.io | awk '{print $1}')
kubectl delete crd $(kubectl get crd | grep toolkit.fluxcd.io | awk '{print $1}')
```

uninstall.title: Remove the namespace
uninstall.description:
Delete the 'flux-system' namespace to clean up all resources.
```bash
kubectl delete namespace flux-system
```

upgrade.title: Backup current state
upgrade.description:
Export the current Flux configuration to a YAML file for backup.
```bash
kubectl get all -n flux-system -o yaml > flux-backup.yaml
```

upgrade.title: Update the Helm repository
upgrade.description:
Update the Helm repository to get the latest charts.
```bash
helm repo update
```

upgrade.title: Upgrade Flux
upgrade.description:
Upgrade the Flux installation to the latest version.
```bash
helm upgrade flux fluxcd-community/flux2 --namespace flux-system
```

upgrade.title: Verify the upgrade
upgrade.description:
Check if the Flux pods are running the new version successfully.
```bash
kubectl get pods --namespace flux-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Pods stuck in CrashLoopBackOff
troubleshooting.description:
This can happen due to misconfigurations or resource limits. Check the logs for the pod to diagnose the issue.
```bash
kubectl logs <pod-name> -n flux-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Flux not syncing with Git repository
troubleshooting.description:
Ensure that the Git repository URL and credentials are correctly configured. Check the Flux source-controller logs for errors.
```bash
kubectl logs -l app=source-controller -n flux-system
```

troubleshooting.title: Helm release failed
troubleshooting.description:
If the Helm release fails, check the events in the namespace for more details.
```bash
kubectl get events -n flux-system
```

resolution.summary: A successful installation of Flux will have all pods in the 'flux-system' namespace running without errors. You should be able to see the Flux controllers (source-controller, kustomize-controller, helm-controller, notification-controller) managing your Kubernetes resources as per your Git configurations.
codeSnippets:
helm repo add fluxcd-community https://fluxcd-community.github.io/helm-charts
helm repo update

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running and both Helm and kubectl installed on your local machine.
